challenges.title: Vector Class
challenges.description:
Create a class `Vector` with `__init__(self, x, y)` that stores x and y coordinates.

Implement these operator overloads:
- `__add__(self, other)` — vector addition, returns a new Vector(self.x + other.x, self.y + other.y)
- `__mul__(self, scalar)` — scalar multiplication, returns a new Vector(self.x * scalar, self.y * scalar)
- `__len__(self)` — returns 2 (a 2D vector always has 2 components)
- `__repr__(self)` — returns "Vector({x}, {y})"
challenges.test_code:
v1 = Vector(1, 2)
v2 = Vector(3, 4)

assert repr(v1) == "Vector(1, 2)", f"repr should be 'Vector(1, 2)', got '{repr(v1)}'"
assert repr(v2) == "Vector(3, 4)", f"repr should be 'Vector(3, 4)', got '{repr(v2)}'"

v3 = v1 + v2
assert repr(v3) == "Vector(4, 6)", f"v1 + v2 should be Vector(4, 6), got {repr(v3)}"
assert isinstance(v3, Vector), "Addition should return a Vector instance"

v4 = v1 * 3
assert repr(v4) == "Vector(3, 6)", f"v1 * 3 should be Vector(3, 6), got {repr(v4)}"
assert isinstance(v4, Vector), "Multiplication should return a Vector instance"

assert len(v1) == 2, f"len(Vector) should be 2, got {len(v1)}"

# Verify originals unchanged
assert repr(v1) == "Vector(1, 2)", "Original vector should not be modified"
print("All tests passed!")
hints: __add__ should return Vector(self.x + other.x, self.y + other.y) — a NEW vector, not modifying self.
__mul__ receives a scalar (integer or float): return Vector(self.x * scalar, self.y * scalar).
__len__ must return an integer. For a 2D vector, just return 2.

challenges.title: Smart List
challenges.description:
Create a class `SmartList` that wraps a regular Python list.

Implement these methods:
- `__init__(self, data)` — stores the list as an instance attribute
- `__getitem__(self, index)` — returns the item at the given index
- `__len__(self)` — returns the length of the internal list
- `__contains__(self, item)` — returns True if item is in the list
- `__add__(self, other)` — returns a new SmartList combining both lists (other is also a SmartList)
challenges.test_code:
sl = SmartList([1, 2, 3, 4, 5])
assert len(sl) == 5, f"len should be 5, got {len(sl)}"
assert sl[0] == 1, f"sl[0] should be 1, got {sl[0]}"
assert sl[4] == 5, f"sl[4] should be 5, got {sl[4]}"
assert sl[-1] == 5, f"sl[-1] should be 5, got {sl[-1]}"

assert 3 in sl, "3 should be in the SmartList"
assert 99 not in sl, "99 should not be in the SmartList"

sl2 = SmartList([6, 7, 8])
sl3 = sl + sl2
assert isinstance(sl3, SmartList), "Addition should return a SmartList"
assert len(sl3) == 8, f"Combined length should be 8, got {len(sl3)}"
assert sl3[5] == 6, f"sl3[5] should be 6, got {sl3[5]}"
assert sl3[7] == 8, f"sl3[7] should be 8, got {sl3[7]}"

# Verify originals unchanged
assert len(sl) == 5, "Original SmartList should not be modified"
assert len(sl2) == 3, "Other SmartList should not be modified"
print("All tests passed!")
hints: Store the list as self.data in __init__. All other methods delegate to self.data.
__getitem__ just returns self.data[index]; Python handles negative indexing automatically.
For __add__, combine the internal lists: return SmartList(self.data + other.data).
